PROJ_DEV_OBJECTIVE_DESC: The project aims to empower the rural poor in Sierra Leone to increase their food security and incomes on a sustainable basis to lead to long-term economic development and poverty reduction. It focuses on reducing the gap between national rice production and demand by 70,000 metric tons and increasing farm incomes by 10 percent for direct beneficiaries. The project targets 100,000 direct and 300,000 indirect beneficiaries, specifically smallholder farmers, women, and youth. Expected outcomes include the intensification of rice production on 4,000 hectares of Inland Valley Swamps and the rehabilitation of 10,000 hectares of tree crops.
Indicators: Reduced gap between national rice production and demand (70,000 metric tons) | Rice upland yields increased by 15 percent | Intensification of rice production on 4,000 ha of Inland Valley Swamps | Rice post-harvest losses reduced by 20 percent | Farm incomes increased by 10 percent for direct beneficiaries | 100,000 direct and 300,000 indirect beneficiaries | 10,000 ha of tree crops rehabilitation | 15 Financial Service Associations and 7 Community Banks (CB) able to deliver rural finance | 341 functional agricultural input suppliers
Components: Component 1: Smallholder Agriculture and Commercialization - increase productivity, rural incomes and employment through better access to technical skills, services and markets | Component 2: Small Scale Irrigation - boost rice production through the rehabilitation of Inland Valley Swamps (IVS) | Component 3: Rural Finance - provide better access to financial services specifically tailored to rural farming groups through FSAs and Community Banks | Component 4: Coordination and Management - support for planning, coordination, and monitoring and evaluation of the programme.
